# paper7

> CLI tool that fetches arXiv papers as clean markdown for LLM knowledge bases. Zero dependencies beyond curl/sed/grep/awk.

## Usage

### Search papers by keyword
```
paper7 search "attention mechanism"
paper7 search "reinforcement learning" --max 20
paper7 search "transformer architecture" --sort date
```

### Fetch paper as markdown
```
paper7 get 2401.04088
paper7 get https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.04088
paper7 get 2401.04088 --no-refs
paper7 get 2401.04088 --no-cache
```

### Find GitHub repository for a paper
```
paper7 repo 2401.04088
```

### List cached papers (local KB)
```
paper7 list
```

### Manage cache
```
paper7 cache clear
paper7 cache clear 2401.04088
```

## Commands

- `search <query>` — Search arXiv papers by keyword. Options: `--max N` (default 10), `--sort relevance|date`
- `get <id>` — Fetch paper from ar5iv, convert to clean markdown, cache locally. Options: `--no-refs` (strip references), `--no-cache` (force re-download)
- `repo <id>` — Extract GitHub repository URLs from paper
- `list` — Show all cached papers with ID and title
- `cache clear [id]` — Clear all cache or remove specific paper

## Notes

- Papers are fetched from ar5iv.labs.arxiv.org (HTML version of arXiv)
- Cached papers are stored in `~/.paper7/cache/<id>/paper.md`
- Like context7 but for academic papers
- Output is plain text to stdout, suitable for piping
- Accepts arXiv ID (2401.04088) or full URL (https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.04088)
